message:
perf(transport): eliminate per-dispatch Vec allocations with SmallVec and lazy endpoint filtering

DispatchResult optimization:
- sent_to: Vec<EndpointId> → SmallVec<[EndpointId; 4]>
- failed_endpoints: Vec<(EndpointId, DispatchError)> → SmallVec<[...; 4]>
  Unicast dispatches (the overwhelmingly common case) now stay fully
  inline on the stack, avoiding a heap allocation per dispatch. All
  construction sites updated to use smallvec![] and SmallVec::new().

LoadBalancer lazy filtering:
- RoundRobin, Random, HashBased, FirstAvailable, LeastConnections,
  WeightedLeastConnections: replaced upfront Vec::collect() of available
  endpoints with lazy .filter().nth() / .filter().min_by_key() chains.
  This avoids a Vec allocation per select() call — the filter is
  evaluated lazily against the endpoint slice.
- WeightedRoundRobin: still needs to materialize (for weight summing),
  but now uses SmallVec<[&Arc<Endpoint>; 8]> for inline small clusters.

RoutingTable.lookup_without_default():
- New method that performs object/region compound-key fallback without
  ever consulting the default route. SymbolRouter.route() and
  route_multicast() use this for primary lookup, then explicitly check
  allow_fallback before trying RouteKey::Default. This makes fallback
  opt-in and correctly sets is_fallback=true only for default-route hits.

dispatch_multicast early return:
- count==0 now returns an empty success result immediately instead of
  proceeding to route lookup and endpoint selection.

New tests:
- test_symbol_router_without_fallback: verifies without_fallback()
  rejects default-only routes
- dispatch_result_unicast_stays_inline: asserts SmallVec doesn't spill
  for single-endpoint dispatches

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
